economic_notes: Band is economically dependent on Indigenous Services Canada Comprehensive Funding Arrangement and First Nations Health Authority funding. COVID-19 noted as potential operational risk.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021, not 2024. One consolidated subsidiary: Gitwangak Education Society. No GBEs. Trust Funds held by Federal Government ($430,519) included as financial assets. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($6,563,720) + FNHA ($1,660,840) + Province of BC ($600,000) + FNESC ($424,878) + CMHC ($23,653) + New Relationship Trust ($24,446) + First Peoples Heritage Fund ($43,564) + BC Aboriginal Childcare Society ($45,466) + Tricorp ($73,095) + Property tax ($30,592) + Replacement Reserve Fund amounts excluded = $9,490,254 approximately; OSR includes rental revenue, other revenue, interest, and admin fees. Annual debt service includes principal reduction of long-term debt ($11,936) + repayment of demand loan ($86,039) = $97,975. No schedule of remuneration provided. Capital Fund expenses ($457,435) included in total but not mapped to a standard category above.
